prevalence.source: NMHS/ICMR + IHME GBD with subtype estimates

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: hypomanic episodes + major depressive episodes; no full manic episode

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to treatment gaps and limited follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often misdiagnosed as unipolar depression; specialist confirmation concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Mood stabilizers and atypical antipsychotics; psychoeducation; avoid antidepressant monotherapy when possible
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Specialist scarcity and high stigma with limited psychotherapy support
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generics and expanding tertiary psychiatry services in major cities
